data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, corroborated against the repo.

RESOLVED since prior G4 audits on this ticket (NOT re-filed): the earlier 33d0↔2359 "ordering violation / which-path-survives" finding rested on the absence of a sequencing edge. Sibling 2359 now declares `depends_on 33d0` (present in 2359 deps; restated in its description "Sequencing: depends on story D (the single consolidated applier) so the counts come from one path"). 33d0 collapses to ONE production path FIRST, then 2359 instruments the single survivor — the same-region co-edit of batch_dispatch.py/applier.py is now sequenced, not concurrent. The ordering-violation mode reads benign under current ticket state.

ONE high-confidence finding remains that the depends_on edge does NOT resolve — a representation conflict on the SHARED outbound-payload contract that 33d0 owns and 2359 builds its canary over:

FINDING — conflicting assumptions: 33d0 owns the surviving applier's per-sub-op payload, in which `labels_applied` is list-valued while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints; downstream sibling 2359's `computed > 0 && applied == 0` count-canary assumes a uniform integer count, and 33d0 fixes no canonical label representation for the survivor.
- finding: 33d0 (What) "Retarget the existing typed-leaf tests at the surviving production path" plus its parity gate ("assert the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did") make 33d0 the owner of the surviving applier's per-sub-op result payload. Repo-confirmed, that payload is NOT uniformly count-shaped: in the typed leaf `_apply_outbound_update` (the path 33d0 may delete or promote), `labels_applied` is a `list[str]` of decorated label strings (apply_outbound.py L245/L257/L260/L389) while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints (L325/L278). The existing typed-leaf tests 33d0 retargets assert the EXACT list contents of `labels_applied` (test_applier_outbound_update.py L204 `== ["+tag-alpha","+tag-beta"]`, L233 `== ["-stale-tag"]`). Sibling 2359 (depends_on 33d0) then surfaces `labels_applied` in the SAME count-and-invariant frame as links/comments and defines a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` canary citing "parity with the inbound applier's existing links_applied" (an int — apply_inbound.py). 33d0 nowhere states the canonical label representation for the single surviving payload, so the survivor 2359 instruments is handed an ambiguous label signal: keep the list (2359's `applied == 0` predicate is ill-typed over a list — `[]` ≠ `0`, a non-empty list is truthy) or flip to an int (breaks the exact-list asserts 33d0 just retargeted). Either way a shared contract is left unowned at the seam between THIS child (owns the survivor's payload) and 2359 (consumes it for the canary).
  - criteria: 33d0 AC1 (exactly one applier path; retired path deleted), 33d0 AC2 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE — fields+comments+labels+links+parent — asserting each sub-op took effect), 33d0 parity gate (surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did); cross-checked against 2359 AC1 (surface labels_applied) + 2359 AC2/AC5 (computed>0 && applied==0 count canary); parent AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path" (33d0) vs parent AC "computed > 0 && applied == 0 fails loudly" (2359)
  - location: 33d0 What/Scope (retarget typed-leaf tests; parity gate over the surviving payload); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py L245 (`labels_applied: list[str] = []`), L257/L260 (`.append(f"+{label_name}")`/`f"-{label_name}"`), L389 (`"labels_applied": labels_applied`) vs L278/L325 (`comments_applied`/`links_applied` int); batch_dispatch.py update_one (the production batch path applies labels/comments/links but tracks NO per-sub-op counter today — the survivor 33d0 must reconcile against the typed-leaf shape); apply_inbound.py inbound `links_applied` int (the parity model 2359 cites); tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_applier_outbound_update.py L204/L233 (exact-list asserts on labels_applied) — the tests 33d0 retargets
  - evidence: repo confirms the surviving-applier candidate payload carries `labels_applied` as list[str] and links/comments_applied as ints; the existing tests 33d0 retargets assert exact label-list contents (not a count); the production batch path 33d0 may keep has NO per-sub-op counter at all, so 33d0's parity gate must MINT the label signal's representation for the survivor; 2359 (depends_on 33d0) then layers a count-based `computed>0 && applied==0` invariant on that very key citing an int parity model; the dual path itself is repo-confirmed (typed-leaf route reconcile.py L75 / typed_dispatch.py L56 vs batch route batch_dispatch.update_one, whose comment at L388 notes links/labels flow "through this legacy batch path, NOT the typed leaf")
  - scenarios: (a) 33d0 promotes/retargets keeping `labels_applied` as a list to satisfy its retargeted exact-list tests → 2359's `applied == 0` canary over labels mis-fires or never fires (`[]` ≠ `0`; non-empty list truthy), so the silent-no-op guarantee silently fails for the labels sub-op specifically while links/comments are covered. (b) 33d0 (or 2359) normalizes `labels_applied` to an int → the exact-list assertions at test_applier_outbound_update.py L204/L233 that 33d0 retargeted break → parent "no capability regresses; full reconciler suite green" (33d0 AC4) regresses. (c) 33d0 leaves the representation unstated → the surviving-applier payload contract (33d0) and the canary (2359) disagree on which field/shape the invariant reads.
  - impact: 33d0's parity gate and retargeted tests cannot be checked off consistently with 2359's count-canary unless 33d0 fixes the surviving payload's label representation; a silent risk to parent SC "a silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green" (holds for links/comments but possibly not labels) and to parent "suite green" (33d0 AC4) if the list key is flipped.
  - checklist_item: cross-child modes — conflicting assumptions (shared outbound-payload representation between 33d0's surviving applier and 2359's count-canary)
  - suggested_fix: in 33d0, fix the surviving consolidated applier's per-sub-op payload representation explicitly — keep the existing list-valued `labels_applied` AND expose a derived count (e.g. `labels_applied_count` / `len(labels_applied)`) so the exact-list tests survive while a count exists for the invariant — and state that one representation in 33d0's parity/AC so 2359's `computed > 0 && applied == 0` predicate is well-typed over labels by construction across the single production path.

SECONDARY (recorded under implicit-shared-state; the depends_on largely tames it, not separately filed): 33d0 and 2359 co-edit the SAME link/comment/label dispatch region of batch_dispatch.py update_one and the applier.py RECON batch_outcome line. 2359's depends_on 33d0 sequences the work so 33d0 lands first; the residual is only that 33d0 carries no reciprocal note that 2359 will re-touch the same region to add counters/canary, and 33d0 should leave the surviving dispatch loop structured so per-sub-op counts thread in without re-architecting it — folded into the suggested_fix above.

Modes that read BENIGN (not filed): ordering violation — RESOLVED via 2359 depends_on 33d0. Dependency gap (on inputs) — 33d0 is pure outbound-code consolidation needing no schema/fixture input, so unlike 822a/59ef (which correctly declare depends_on fe57) its lack of an input dep is appropriate. Scope overlap — 33d0 AC2 (outbound-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect) vs 822a AC1 (bidirectional inbound↔outbound round-trip) vs 2359 AC2 (computed>0&&applied==0 canary) are distinct intents over the same production path; 33d0 declares telemetry OUT (→2359) consistently. Consumer impact — once 33d0 collapses to one path, 8937 AC3 / 822a AC1 (which drive "the production path") target the survivor by construction; benign given 33d0 lands first per the dependency graph. Residual references — 33d0 explicitly deletes the dead typed leaf AND its now-misleading tests (33d0 AC1 + the parity-before-delete gate), so it does not strand references; siblings 8937/fe57/59ef/822a key on the inbound snapshot/fetch surface or drive the production path, not the deleted typed leaf.

The roster-level parent-AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) matter is a G3 coverage question — and is in any case owned by story B/8937 per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.
